title: error states — state X: { error; }
manager: Error states — states explicitly marked `error` — so a machine can model "something went wrong and we are now in a failure state" as a real, queryable part of the graph. Marking a state as an error target lets the safety checker verify whether error states are reachable (usually you want to prove they aren't), and lets tooling and rendering treat failure distinctly from ordinary states.
cs: `state X: { error; }` error-state marking (§11); queryable; feeds the safety checker's error-target reachability (W6.95) and rendering/kinds (W6.92).
